Resolves #10256 by enabling autoSave in PermissionService spec so SQLite state is flushed before tests execute direct SQL assertions.

## Comments

### `@neo-opus-4-7` commented on 2026-04-23T22:03:50Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # PR Review Summary
> 
> **Status:** Approved
> 
> **Peer-Review Opening:** Clean chore fix. The `autoSave = true` + serial-mode discipline matches the `MailboxService.spec` pattern we converged on for this directory, and the save/restore via `originalAutoSave` is the correct hygiene for singleton-state-mutation. Shipping.
>

> 
> ### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
> *   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 95 — 5 points deducted because the fix mechanism (`autoSave = true` to flush SQLite state before the two failing tests assert direct SQL shape) isn't inline-commented. Future readers will see the `autoSave` toggle and wonder why this spec needs it when others don't — an Anchor & Echo line linking the discipline to the "direct SQL assertion" context would make the intent auditable.
> *   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 85 — 15 points deducted because the PR body is a one-liner. I recognize this is a chore-scoped one-pattern fix, but even chore PRs benefit from a "what was failing / why this closes it" shape (even 3 sentences). The ticket body carries the detail; the PR body could quote or link the relevant root-cause hypothesis.
> *   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 90 — 10 points split: (a) includes the `.sync-metadata.json` timestamp churn in the diff (tobi flagged this as a systematic gh-workflow server issue — filed as #10267 for investigation); (b) no explicit regression-test evidence in PR body (a `npm run test-unit -- ...PermissionService.spec.mjs` output showing 7/7 passing would close out the AC empirically).
> *   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 100 — Closes #10256 exactly. No scope creep.
> *   **`[IMPACT]`**: 20 — Test-infrastructure hygiene; very low product impact but restores coverage for `PermissionService.grantPermission`'s #10231 guard behavior.
> *   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 10 — Trivial: serial-mode configure + two variable lines + three touchpoints.
> *   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Maintenance — targeted hygiene chore.

> ### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
> *   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #10256
> *   **Related Graph Nodes:** `Neo.ai.mcp.server.memory-core.services.PermissionService`, #10229 (test pollution refactor — this closes the remaining #10231-era coverage gap), #10231 (grantPermission existence guard — the test at line 147 explicitly covers this)
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes
> 
> *   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: The `autoSave = true` + `test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' })` pattern is now the third Memory Core spec to need it (`MailboxService.spec`, now `PermissionService.spec`, presumably will also be needed on any future spec that asserts via `db.storage.db.prepare(...).get(...)` style direct-SQL reads). Worth naming the pattern explicitly in a shared test-infrastructure doc or the `unit-test` skill reference: **"any spec that asserts via direct SQLite queries against the graph MUST pin `autoSave = true` during the suite + restore in `afterAll`, and MUST use `serial` mode to avoid parallel workers interleaving SQLite-flush state."** Prevents re-derivation when the fourth spec hits the same issue.
> 
> ---
> 
> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Documented search**: I actively looked for (a) whether the two previously-failing tests have specific root causes distinguishable enough to warrant separate fixes (no — both fail from the same shared-state-flush-before-assertion cause, single fix closes both), (b) whether the `autoSave` toggle could leak state to other specs running in parallel (no — `afterAll` restores the original value; worst case is a worker-ordering artifact that this spec's own `serial` mode contains), (c) whether the broader `GraphService.db.autoSave` default value should change globally (no — default `false` is correct for production paths where writes batch; spec-level override is the right scope). Found no concerns.
>
